scenario_name: Depp vs Heard Trial Public Opinion 2022
description: Johnny Depp's defamation trial against Amber Heard (April-June 2022) became a massive social media spectacle. Pro-Depp sentiment surged from roughly even to ~85% as trial evidence and social media dynamics overwhelmingly favored Depp. Tests social media amplification and bandwagon effects.

key_events.description: Trial begins live-streamed on April 11. Depp's testimony about abuse allegations generates massive social media engagement and sympathy.
Audio recordings of Heard admitting to hitting Depp go viral. TikTok pro-Depp content dominates algorithm. #JusticeForJohnnyDepp trends globally.
Heard's testimony widely mocked on social media. Credibility-damaging moments (dog stepping on a bee, donation vs pledge) become viral memes.
Closing arguments. Overwhelming social media consensus pro-Depp. Bot analysis suggests some amplification but organic support is dominant.
Jury awards Depp $10.35M in damages, Heard $2M. Verdict aligns with public opinion. YouGov poll shows 85% siding with Depp.

calibration_notes: Tests extreme social media bandwagon and platform amplification effects. Key patterns: one of the most lopsided public opinion outcomes in modern trial history, TikTok algorithm creating echo chamber and meme cascade, live-streaming enabling parasocial engagement, pre-existing celebrity affection advantage for Depp, gender politics counter-narrative (Heard supporters framed it as #MeToo backlash) failed to gain traction, ~35pt swing over 6 weeks. YouGov, Morning Consult polling data used.
